message:
test(fp-io): extend SQL fuzz target to cover indexed reads + consistent empty-string rejection (frankenpandas-cod2-79f08a56)

The frankenpandas SQL fuzz target previously dispatched only into
fp_io::read_sql. The other public SQL entrypoints
(read_sql_query, read_sql_with_options, read_sql_table_with_index_col,
read_sql_with_index_col, etc.) accept untrusted index_col / query
arguments and route through different validation logic — so any
panic latent in the explicit index_col path was untested. Beads
issue frankenpandas-cod2-79f08a56 (priority 2, type bug, label
testing-fuzzing/fp-io/sql/parity) captures the diagnosis.

Three correlated changes:

1) crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs — consistent empty-string rejection on
   index_col

   sql_indexed_chunks already rejected an index_col not present in
   result columns but was missing the leading is_empty() check that
   SqlReadOptions.index_col validation performs at the options-level
   API. The explicit index_col call sites
   (read_sql_with_index_col, read_sql_table_with_index_col,
   read_sql_query_with_options_and_index_col) bypassed the
   options-level path and therefore the empty-string check, allowing
   "" to slip through to promote_column_to_index where it would
   surface a confusing "column not found" error instead of the
   intended "empty string is not a valid column name".

   Fix: route every explicit-index_col code path through
   apply_sql_index_col (already had the empty-string + lookup
   validation), making promote_column_to_index strictly an internal
   helper called by apply_sql_index_col rather than directly from
   the public API. SqlIndexedChunkIterator::next now also funnels
   through apply_sql_index_col so chunked reads honor the same
   rejection rule. Add the missing is_empty() check in
   sql_indexed_chunks itself for the chunk-iterator path.

2) crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs — fuzz_read_sql_bytes dispatch
   matrix

   Update the fuzz harness signature to keep legacy bare-bytes
   inputs working while also recognizing a [0xff, mode, rest...]
   prefix that selects which SQL API to call. The mode byte's low
   bits select the API entrypoint (read_sql / read_sql_query /
   read_sql_with_options / read_sql_table_with_index_col / ...);
   bit 3 (0b1000) toggles whether index_col is set in
   SqlReadOptions; the index_col itself is derived from
   fuzz_sql_index_col(mode) so the fuzzer can produce empty-string,
   nonexistent-column, and present-column variants deterministically.

   Successful frames are checked against length / column-count /
   index-length invariants so a successful return from a previously-
   panicking input still surfaces as a structural assertion failure
   rather than silent acceptance.

3) crates/fp-conformance/src/tests/fuzz_seed_fixtures.rs — three
   regression seeds

   - fuzz_read_sql_bytes_accepts_indexed_query_dispatch_seed:
     [0xff, 0x15, "SELECT a, b FROM t1 ORDER BY a"] — exercises
     the read_sql_query indexed path.
   - fuzz_read_sql_bytes_accepts_empty_index_col_dispatch_seed:
     [0xff, 0x35, "SELECT a, b FROM t1 ORDER BY a"] — empty
     index_col, must report the new "empty string is not a valid
     column name" error and not panic.
   - fuzz_read_sql_bytes_accepts_indexed_table_dispatch_seed:
     [0xff, 0x26, "x"] — read_sql_table_with_index_col with a
     1-byte table name.

   Each seed is just a smoke-corpus check; the actual fuzz coverage
   continues to come from the libfuzzer harness corpus, which now
   has these mode bits to explore.

Beads frankenpandas-cod2-79f08a56 stays in_progress; full corpus
sweep + 12-hour soak under the new dispatch is the remaining
acceptance item.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
